name: Padmapada
dates_notes: Direct disciple of Śaṅkara per tradition; only the first few pādas of his Pañcapādikā survive.
core_thesis: Padmapāda, Śaṅkara's direct disciple, is the founding figure of what becomes the *Vivaraṇa* sub-school of Advaita. He develops the *adhyāsa-bhāṣya* and the opening sūtras of the Brahma-Sūtra into a systematic positive ontology of *avidyā* (also called *māyā* and *mūla-prakṛti*): *avidyā* is a positive, beginningless (*anādi*) *bhāva-rūpa* (existence-natured) entity — not mere absence of knowledge — whose locus (*āśraya*) and object (*viṣaya*) are both *brahman*. *Avidyā* possesses two *śaktis*: *āvaraṇa* (concealment of *brahman*'s svarūpa) and *vikṣepa* (projection of the manifest world). The reflected-consciousness account of the *jīva* (*pratibimba-vāda*) — the *jīva* as a reflection (*pratibimba*) of *brahman* in the mirror of *avidyā* — originates here and is the structural alternative to Maṇḍana's *avaccheda-vāda* (limitation account) inherited by the *Bhāmatī* line. Padmapāda's positions on *avidyā*'s positivity, on *brahman*'s being its substrate, and on *pratibimba* are foundational for Prakāśātman's *Vivaraṇa* and the entire later *Vivaraṇa* tradition.

engaged_works.title: Pancapadika
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored for the surviving portion (first five pādas of Śaṅkara's Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya).

ascription_evidence.description: Modern academic consensus on Padmapāda's authorship of the surviving portion.

engaged_works.summary: *Ṭīkā* on Śaṅkara's Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya. The text-historical situation is critical: only the portion covering the first five *pādas* of the BSB survives — that is, the *adhyāsa-bhāṣya* and the *catuḥsūtrī* (BSB 1.1.1–4) plus a small additional stretch (the work's title literally means 'commentary on five *pādas*'). The traditional account holds that Padmapāda completed the *ṭīkā* on the entire BSB but the manuscript was destroyed; what survives is what was reconstructed from oral memory by Śaṅkara himself. Whatever the historical truth of that narrative, the surviving portion is doctrinally weight-bearing because it covers exactly the BSB's metaphysically densest passages — the *adhyāsa-bhāṣya* and *tat tu samanvayāt*. Padmapāda's elaboration of *avidyā* as *bhāva-rūpa*, his *pratibimba-vāda*, and his analysis of *adhyāsa* are taken up wholesale by Prakāśātman in the *Pañcapādikā-Vivaraṇa*, which becomes the founding text of the *Vivaraṇa* sub-school. Securely authored for the surviving portion.
